notes: The campaign completed all 40 batches on their first attempt. All 180 packet events and all 12 continuous windows were captured; PPK2 integrity is independent of RF delivery status.

Continuous RX delivery at -18 dBm was effectively perfect at all rates. At -6 and 0 dBm, loss reached 40.5767% and 34.7800% at 250 kbps, and 21.4246% and 27.2370% at 1 Mbps. All three 2 Mbps points had zero loss.

A 36-scenario serial-only diagnostic reproduced the power/rate dependence in both directions and on channels 80 and 40 with zero transmitter serial errors, excluding PPK2 and the web campaign as the cause.
Both radios reported the expected RF_SETUP values 0x21, 0x25, and 0x27 for 250 kbps at -18, -6, and 0 dBm, so the requested power settings reached the transceiver registers correctly.

The leading hardware hypotheses are insufficient local supply decoupling or non-ideal RF-module implementation at higher TX power. Nordic's product specification recommends close high-performance VDD decoupling, a parallel approximately 4.7 uF ceramic capacitor, filtered supply routing, and short supply connections.

The initial four-step quick check sampled only high-power/fast-rate and low-power/slow-rate corners, so it could not expose this interaction. The nRF24L01 quick check now adds high-power/slow-rate TX and RX cases and uses the full 32-byte physical frame.

The current dataset is retained as the authoritative characterization of the connected modules and bench layout. A new named campaign is recommended after adding local decoupling or increasing RF separation; it should not overwrite these measurements.

Post-validation cleanup removed eight non-authoritative quick-check raw traces (2,867,760 bytes) and duplicate source copies of seven diagnostic logs plus the sustained-diagnostic directory. Their compact results and verified log copies remain under this comparison; all 192 authoritative campaign traces remain present.
